description: Document the hosted Trustgrid MCP server so public users can connect safely, understand the scoped tool groups, and reference every public top-level MCP tool with accurate permissions and sample interactions.

userStories.title: Document the hosted MCP transport and public endpoints
userStories.description: As an MCP client user, I need clear documentation for the public Trustgrid MCP transport and endpoint layout so I can connect without guessing whether the service is stdio, SSE, or remote Streamable HTTP.
acceptanceCriteria: Document that the hosted Trustgrid MCP service uses remote Streamable HTTP over HTTPS
Document the production endpoint and scoped MCP paths
Clarify that the hosted service does not use legacy HTTP SSE transport
Document the recommended starting endpoint for first-time users
userStories.notes: The MCP landing, reference, and client pages now point users at the hosted HTTPS endpoint, explain the scoped path layout, and recommend starting with /mcp/read.

userStories.title: Document the public MCP tool groups and read-first workflow
userStories.description: As a new Trustgrid MCP user, I want to understand what each public tool group exposes so I can start with the safer read/query surface before using broader workflows or live diagnostics.
acceptanceCriteria: Describe the public read, codemode, and tools groups
Explain how scoped MCP paths can be combined
Recommend starting with /mcp/read for discovery and inspection
Link the high-level group summary to the detailed tool reference
userStories.notes: The MCP landing page and tools-and-groups page now lead with the read surface, explain combined scope paths, and point users to the detailed tool reference.

userStories.title: Publish a complete public MCP tool reference
userStories.description: As an MCP user or integration author, I need a complete reference for every public top-level MCP tool so I can understand what each tool does, what permissions it needs, and what a realistic request/response looks like.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a table-based reference covering every public top-level MCP tool exposed by the hosted service
Include columns for tool name, brief description, permissions required, and a realistic sample interaction
Exclude switchOrg because it is stdio-only/internal and not part of the hosted public MCP surface
Document that codemode helper functions inside code execution are not separate top-level MCP tools
Validate the published tool inventory against trustgrid/mcp source registrations
userStories.notes: content/en/docs/mcp/tool-reference/index.md now documents 50 public top-level tools: 4 codemode tools from registerCodemodeTools, 36 direct read tools from READ_ONLY_OPERATION_IDS, and 10 node diagnostic tools from NODE_TOOLS. The page also explains how permissions were derived from the public spec and scope map.
